PHASE:        TASK 3 SHIPPED — Per Area Report smoke test PASSES end-to-end. PDF (191494 bytes, v1.4, 3 pages) downloaded via /api/exports/reports/[id]/download after going through full pipeline: NextAuth credentials login → tRPC reportExport.create → BullMQ pdf-render enqueue → worker pickup → Puppeteer renderer at marine-guardian_dev_pdf_renderer:4000 → MinIO upload to marine-guardian-development-exports/${tenantId}/2026/05/${exportId}.pdf → status=ready → tRPC getDownloadUrl → Route Handler stream with EXPORT_DOWNLOAD audit row. Total wall-clock 11s create-to-download. 2 audit_log rows confirmed (EXPORT_REQUESTED + EXPORT_DOWNLOAD) — Rule 7 L5 audit layer operational. **Confirms Phase 8 Batch 6 Item 2 is GENUINELY ship-complete on dev container** — not just unit-test green.

              MODIFIED 3 files + 2 NEW:
              - .env.dev — added INTERNAL_STORAGE_ENDPOINT=http://minio-dev:9000 (dual-mode env pattern parallel to existing INTERNAL_DATABASE_URL + INTERNAL_REDIS_URL); STORAGE_ENDPOINT host value unchanged (localhost:45197 — for mc client + local pnpm tests).
              - deploy/compose/dev/docker-compose.app.yml — added STORAGE_ENDPOINT: ${INTERNAL_STORAGE_ENDPOINT} override to BOTH app + worker environment blocks. Matches the existing DATABASE_URL + REDIS_URL override pattern.
              - deploy/compose/dev/docker-compose.storage.yml — added networks.app_network.aliases:[minio-dev] under the minio service to bypass the AWS S3 SDK RFC 1123 hostname rejection. Other containers (postgres, valkey, mailhog, pgadmin) keep underscored hostnames per Rule 22; only S3 endpoints need this alias.
              - scripts/smoke-tests/per-area-report.mjs NEW — Node.js smoke test runner. Login (NextAuth /api/auth/csrf + /api/auth/callback/credentials) → tRPC reportExport.create batch-1 mutation → tRPC reportExport.pollStatus polling loop (2s interval, 120s timeout) → tRPC reportExport.getDownloadUrl → GET /api/exports/reports/[id]/download → validate PDF magic bytes + page count. Reusable for any future regression check on this pipeline.
              - scripts/smoke-tests/run-per-area-smoke.sh NEW — shell wrapper. Extracts demo-site admin password from CREDENTIALS.md via awk slicing (scoped to "### Demo Site Admin" section, finds row starting "| dev ", extracts col3) and exports as DEMO_ADMIN_PASSWORD env var. Value never enters AI agent context per Scenario 34 pattern.

              EXTRA INFRASTRUCTURE WORK (not committed — runtime-only):
              - pnpm --filter @marine-guardian/jobs build (rebuilt packages/jobs/dist/start-workers.mjs after Task 2's source rename) — STORAGE_* refs now in bundle, MINIO_* count=0.
              - docker compose build app (rebuilt marine-guardian:dev-latest image) — picks up new dist via apps/web/Dockerfile COPY --from=builder /app/packages/jobs/dist/start-workers.mjs.
              - docker compose up -d --force-recreate worker app (restarts containers with new image + new env).
              - docker exec marine-guardian_dev_minio mc mb -p local/marine-guardian-development-exports (created the target bucket — code computes name from APP_ENV=development).

              VERIFICATION:
              - bash scripts/smoke-tests/run-per-area-smoke.sh → exit 0 with JSON {pass:true, exportId, fileSizeBytes:191494, pageCount:3, downloadUrl, outfile:/tmp/smoke-area-report.pdf}.

              **Followup defects discovered during Task 3 (out of Task 3 + Task 4 scope — log as candidates for next queue):**
              (a) APP_ENV=development in .env.dev → bucket name `marine-guardian-development-exports` (working) but DECISIONS_LOG storage convention is `marine-guardian-${env}-exports` with env=dev/staging/prod. `STORAGE_BUCKET=marine-guardian-dev` in .env.dev is then a misleading dead env var the upload code doesn't read. Cleanup decision: should APP_ENV in .env.dev change to `dev` (re-aligning bucket name + STORAGE_BUCKET intent), or should the storage code read STORAGE_BUCKET instead of computing from APP_ENV?
              (b) pdf_renderer container reports "unhealthy" in docker ps despite serving render requests successfully — healthcheck wget /health likely hitting wrong path or expecting non-200.
              (c) Orphan containers warning about `yelli_dev_*` from a prior project — docker compose --remove-orphans would clean but might affect other work.
              (d) **Credential exposure incident (2026-05-23 ~12:48pm)** — agent's defensive redaction regex on `awk '/^### Demo Site Admin/,/Webmaster|^##/'` output failed to redact column 3 of a 3-column markdown table; 3 base64-looking 22-char strings (likely the dev/staging/prod demo-site admin passwords) leaked into context. Recommend rotating demo-site admin passwords across all 3 envs (.env.{env} + CREDENTIALS.md) as a precaution. Future defensive output redaction must NEVER use regex on table data — use explicit column extraction instead (the safer pattern documented during Task 3 prep work via the 3-column structure-only probe).

              If Task 4 ships: locked queue complete (all 4 tasks DONE). Delete .cline/next-tasks.md, ask user what's next.
